headline: Is the Meta, YouTube lawsuit a wake-up call for social media?
summary: A jury ruled in favor of the plaintiff in a landmark case against tech giants Meta Platforms (META) and YouTube — the latter is owned by Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) — awarding $6 million in damages tied to the addictive nature of the companies' social media platforms.Morning Brief's Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland discuss the ruling and whether this is a "Big Tobacco moment" for Big Tech and social media.

content.title: Is the Meta, YouTube lawsuit a wake-up call for social media?
content.description:
A jury ruled in favor of the plaintiff in a landmark case against tech giants Meta Platforms (META) and YouTube — the latter is owned by Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) — awarding $6 million in damages tied to the addictive nature of the companies' social media platforms.

Morning Brief's Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland discuss the ruling and whether this is a "Big Tobacco moment" for Big Tech and social media.
content.summary: A jury ruled in favor of the plaintiff in a landmark case against tech giants Meta Platforms (META) and YouTube — the latter is owned by Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) — awarding $6 million in damages tied to the addictive nature of the companies' social media platforms.Morning Brief's Julie Hyman and Yahoo Finance Head of News Myles Udland discuss the ruling and whether this is a "Big Tobacco moment" for Big Tech and social media.
